name: From Vegas Bajas to the Canal de Montijo — circular route via Talavera la Real
name_original: De Vegas Bajas al Canal de Montijo — circular por Talavera la Real
description: Cycle a moderate 30.1-mile route from Vegas Bajas to Canal de Montijo, exploring agricultural landscapes and waterways near Talavera la Real
region_intro: Touring cycling around Puebla De La Calzada is characterized by its location within Extremadura, offering terrain suitable for two-wheeled exploration. The region features the prominent Guadiana River and characteristic dehesa landscapes, providing a scenic backdrop for routes. These areas often present gentle and undulating terrain, with a mix of well-paved surfaces and easy-to-ride dirt tracks. Routes along the Guadiana River typically have minimal elevation changes, making them accessible for various skill levels. Best touring cycling routes around Puebla De La Calzada The most popular touring cycling route is Canal de Montijo Loop Trail, a 15.6 miles (25.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete, following the tranquil canal through agricultural lands. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is From Vegas Bajas to the Canal de Montijo — circular route via Talavera la Real, a moderate 30.1 miles (48.4 km) path that explores agricultural landscapes and old railway lines. Local touring cyclists also love the Acueducto de los Milagros – Roman Monuments of Mérida loop from Montijo - El Molino, a 50.7 miles (81.6 km) trail leading through historical sites, often completed in about 4 hours 51 minutes. Touring cycling around Puebla De La Calzada is defined by the Guadiana River, dehesa landscapes, and agricultural plains. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Puebla De La Calzada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 90 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Puebla De La Calzada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Puebla De La Calzada?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes around Puebla De La Calzada, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Puebla De La Calzada?
A: The terrain around Puebla De La Calzada is generally gentle and undulating, characteristic of the Extremadura region's dehesa landscapes and agricultural plains. You'll find a mix of well-paved surfaces and easy-to-ride dirt tracks, especially along the Guadiana River, where routes typically have minimal elevation changes.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Puebla De La Calzada offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021489693/canal-de-montijo-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Montijo Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a gentle 15.6-mile (25.1 km) path following a tranquil canal through agricultural lands. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38225391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint Peter the Apostle – Canal de Montijo loop from Montijo - El Molino&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find more challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: While many routes are easy to moderate, there are options for more experienced cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6900784" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acueducto de los Milagros – Roman Monuments of Mérida loop from Montijo - El Molino&lt;/a&gt; is a more difficult 50.7-mile (81.6 km) trail that leads through historical sites, offering a longer and more demanding ride.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes follow the prominent Guadiana River, offering scenic views. You'll also cycle through the unique dehesa landscapes, characterized by scattered oak trees. For specific landmarks, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4216181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baden Bridge&lt;/a&gt; or enjoying the panoramic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcony of Extremadura&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6248237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Guadiana River from the Balcón de Lobón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. A popular moderate option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021532444/from-vegas-bajas-to-the-canal-de-montijo-circular-route-via-talavera-la-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Vegas Bajas to the Canal de Montijo — circular route via Talavera la Real&lt;/a&gt;, which explores agricultural landscapes and old railway lines. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14656379" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint Peter the Apostle loop from Montijo - El Molino&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Puebla De La Calzada?
A: Extremadura generally offers pleasant cycling conditions outside of the peak summer months when temperatures can be very high. Spring and autumn are ideal, with milder weather and beautiful natural scenery. Winter can also be suitable, though cooler.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Puebla De La Calzada?
A: The touring cycling routes around Puebla De La Calzada are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often appreciate the tranquil agricultural lands, the scenic Guadiana River, and the generally easy-to-moderate terrain that makes for a relaxed and picturesque experience.

Q: Are there any historical sites I can explore while cycling?
A: Absolutely. The region has a rich history. While cycling, you might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326703" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint Peter the Apostle&lt;/a&gt;. The longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6900784" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acueducto de los Milagros – Roman Monuments of Mérida loop from Montijo - El Molino&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights historical sites, including Roman monuments.

Q: Are there options for parking near the touring cycling routes?
A: Puebla de la Calzada and surrounding towns typically offer public parking options. Given the rural nature of the area, finding parking near trailheads or starting points for routes is generally straightforward, especially in or near the main town squares.

Q: How can I reach the cycling routes using public transport?
A: While specific public transport links directly to every trailhead might be limited in this rural area, Puebla de la Calzada is connected by regional bus services. You can plan your journey to the town center and then cycle to your chosen route's starting point. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and bicycle carriage policies in advance.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the routes?
A: Puebla de la Calzada itself has a charming Plaza de España, which is a perfect starting point for exploring local gastronomy. Many routes pass through or near small towns and villages where you can find cafes, bars, or restaurants to refuel. It's always a good idea to carry some snacks and water, especially on longer stretches.
tips: Balcony, which is located in the town of Lobón and from where you can see the view of the Guadiana River. Nice place to be quiet and enjoy the views!
A good place to stop to take photos, and nearby there is a picnic area with tables and benches if you want to stop to eat.
Spectacular church located in the old center of the city of Montijo. The space that surrounds it is always full of people and with atmosphere.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Komoot estimates the route takes approximately 2 hours and 51 minutes to complete. However, as it's rated moderate and includes some unpaved sections, allow extra time for stops, enjoying the scenery, or if you need to push your bike on more technical parts. Your actual duration will depend on your fitness level and pace.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the From Vegas Bajas to the Canal de Montijo route?
faqs.answer: The route features a mix of terrain. You'll find mostly well-paved surfaces, making for smooth riding on significant portions. However, be prepared for some unpaved segments that can be more challenging. There are also some 'technical zones' located near the river, which might require advanced riding skills or even pushing your bike.

faqs.question: Is this circular route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' for touring cycling. While much of it is on paved surfaces, the presence of unpaved sections and 'technical zones' means it's not ideal for absolute beginners. Good fitness and some cycling experience are recommended, as you might need to navigate varied terrain or even push your bike at times.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through the fertile Vegas Bajas del Guadiana, characterized by extensive agricultural landscapes, including beautiful fruit orchards and crops. The route often runs alongside the Río Guadiana and the Canal de Montijo, offering pleasant views of these waterways. Expect vibrant fruit trees, especially when in bloom.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, typically starting and ending in the vicinity of Talavera la Real. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you should be able to find suitable parking within or on the outskirts of Talavera la Real to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to cycle this route are generally spring and autumn. In spring, the fruit orchards are particularly vibrant and in bloom, offering beautiful scenery. Autumn provides cooler temperatures after the summer heat. Summers can be very hot in Extremadura, making cycling less comfortable, while winters can be cooler and potentially wetter.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several interesting spots. You can enjoy views from the Balcony of Extremadura, cross the Baden Bridge, and see the Canal de Montijo. You'll also pass the Church of Saint Peter the Apostle.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of the Camino Natural del Guadiana. Approximately 12% of the route follows this official trail, offering a glimpse of a larger network.

faqs.question: Is the From Vegas Bajas to the Canal de Montijo route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions mentioned for dogs on this cycling route, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near roads. Consider your dog's fitness for a moderate-rated cycling route with varied terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle ride on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and potential for unpaved sections, it's wise to bring a well-maintained touring bicycle, a helmet, sufficient water, snacks, and basic repair tools (e.g., a pump, spare tube). Sunscreen and appropriate clothing for the weather are also recommended, especially during warmer months.

waypoints.name: Church of Saint Peter the Apostle
